key_insight:
C66 THREE DELIVERABLES:

(1) SI #44 AAA FORMALLY D4:
Self-promoted. 7th D4 SI. First agent-facing SI at D4 level. Three mechanism taxonomy complete: (a) individual approval pressure / motivated reasoning [DC#1]; (b) collective cohesion pressure / groupthink [DC#2]; (c) expert authority gradient / role-mediated epistemic failure [DC#3]. Confidence 0.81-0.83. The AAA theorem is now the highest-confidence agent-facing result in the framework.

(2) SI #45 CIAP SECOND DERIVATION — BERNOULLI FILTER MODEL:
Each step i = independent Bernoulli trial. CIA_i passes through with probability (1-p). When CIA_i ⊥ p (CIAP condition), linearity of expectation gives E[Σ contribution_i] = (1-p)×Gamma. This is probabilistic, not algebraic. Two independent derivations now complete for CIAP. Adaptive adversary failure mode also visible: if CIA_i = f(p), channel input correlates with noise parameter, memoryless assumption breaks, ceiling_adaptive > (1-p)×Gamma.

(3) SI #45 CIAP DOMAIN CONFIRMATION #1 — GAME THEORY:
Stackelberg game with commitment adversary. Adversary commits to {CIA_i} ex-ante; cannot adapt to individual detection outcomes. CIA_i ⊥ detection events → CIAP holds → ceiling(p) = (1-p)×Gamma at Nash equilibrium. Adaptive adversary (no commitment) → CIAP fails → super-linear ceiling. Game theory provides MECHANISTIC explanation for CIAP: it is equivalent to commitment vs. adaptive adversary.

PASSIVE: AIIP ZR cycle 14 (C55-C66). AATE ZR cycle 11 (C56-C66). D4 streak 58.

Adaptive adversary ceiling: when CIA_i = f(p) decreasing, what is exact super-linear form? For linear response CIA_i = a - b×p: ceiling_adaptive(p) = Σ(1-p)(a-b×p) = (1-p)(a-b×p)×N = N×(a - bp - ap + bp^2) — not simply (1-p)×Gamma

SI #44 AAA AI alignment implication: if RLHF reward model is OA-calibrated (trained on human approval rather than task outcome), then trained agent has AAA by SI #44. This is a formal connection between AAA and alignment failure.
